name: Rich Canvas / Shader v2

userStory.soThat: I can create beautiful, fluid, and reactive character-art and data visualizations.

acceptanceCriteria: ShaderFn returns a Cell object (char, color, bg) instead of a string
ShaderFn receives normalized UV coordinates (0.0 to 1.0)
Supports 'Braille' and 'Quad' sub-grid scaling for high-resolution plotting
Uniforms registry allows passing external state (time, mouse, theme) into the shader
Braille-scaling automatically maps 2x4 bitmasks to Unicode Braille characters
backwardCompatibility: The legacy canvas() API (string-based) will be preserved as a wrapper around the new engine for simple ASCII art.

testPlan.goldenPath: Write a shader that renders a red circle at (0.5, 0.5) using Braille pixels. Assert that the resulting surface contains the expected Braille characters with correct red foreground colors.
edgeCases: Shaders that return non-character data (invalid cells)
Resolution mismatch between shader and surface
testPlan.fuzzStress: Run a complex Braille shader at 4k resolution (simulated) and measure the time per frame.
failureCases: Floating point drift in UV coordinates affecting reproducible/deterministic output (DIND requirement)

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(W * H * S) where S is sub-grid scale
